title: crotalaria collina
intro: Crotalaria collina, also known as the hill crotalaria, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, classified within the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and flowers, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against cell damage, and prevent infections. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often prepared as a herbal decoction, tea, or powder, and used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and menstrual disorders.
